rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing (Photocopies of the bank statements were finally filed on 11/10/22, but statements for the trust accounts were also filed again in this estate. "Notice of Errata", in addition to not being a proper Probate pleading, amended the pleading caption, and the amendment should have been properly served; unlike Civil, a proof of service attached to a pleading is NOT proof of proper service, which may only be on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 served at least 15 days before a hearing in a decedent's estate.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID L. K. HUANG REVOCABLE TRUST UTD, 09/14/2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/15/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: It appears that the bond should be reduced. The figures in paragraph 3 of the petition do not compute, and the receipts amount is not the amount shown in the attached accounting. Furthermore, there is no reason for receipts for two years to be included in the bond calculation. [CRC 7.903(a)(3) applies here, not just 7.207.] It does not appear prudent for the trustee to pay more in bond premiums than required by law. The order settling the third account, filed on 12/18/20, directed the trustee to obtain two bids if repairs would exceed $10,000, not $20,000. Schedule C of the accounting does not show real property maintenance and repairs disbursements separate from other costs; there should be at least subtotals for each separate category and for each separate real property, not just chronologically for all real property interests together. Was Robert Kearns double paid on 8/27/21? It would be helpful if payments to him (as well as all other disbursements) were shown as a separate subcategory. The trustee should at least disclose the amounts paid to him or for travel, etc., not just claim that the amount was far less than permitted. Was there an order authorizing the allowance to the beneficiary? The trustee also pays for all of his other expenditures: phone, travel, gas, utilities, water, entertainment, gym membership, lots of meals, as well as numerous "personal items"? not more fully described. A miscellaneous withdrawal without more must be deducted from the trustee's fee. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD FLYNN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar and whether the administrator should be suspended for her failure to address the following examiner's notes e-mailed to counsel on 08/15/2022 and resent on 10/10/2022: 1) No proposed order has been received: 2) No DE-120 form with a proof of service is on file. A copy of the new remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) A specific period must be proposed to request a continuance of administration. This issue must be addressed by an amendment or amended petition with new notice. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) An allegation must be made as to whether notice of administration of the estate has been given to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent, together with the expiration date of filing claims. 6) An allegation must be made as to whether any public entity has a claim against the estate under Probate Code section 9201. 7) An allegation must be made as to whether notice under section 9202(a) is required. 8) An allegation must be made as to whether any heir or beneficiary is currently or has previously been confined in any correctional facility described in section 9202(b). 9) The date of mailing notice to the Franchise Tax Board required by section 9202(c) must be specified. 10) An allegation must be made as to whether distribution will be made to any third party such as an agent under a power of attorney or assignee. 11) The inventory and appraisal did not allege the property characterization required by section 8850(c). 12) No election has been filed by the petitioner as the decedent's surviving spouse under section 13502(a). Absent the election, the petitioner's intestate share of the decedent's separate property or his share of community property passed to the petitioner, and the petitioner's share of community property belongs to her, without administration under section 13500. As more than four months have elapsed since letters were issued, a late election under section 13502(b) will require court approval. It should be noted that no spousal property petition has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SARAH ANN CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not the total value of all assets. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Exhibit E should be the list of assets on hand as of a date certain, not just copies of financial statements. Administrator alleges that he did not take any other actions under his IAEA authority, but it appears that stocks were sold and other securities purchased? Nothing in the records indicates that the proposed personal representative, now the administrator, ever disclosed who Decedent's parents were and when they died. [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] This must now be set forth. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The character of a decedent's property is either separate or community, not just "the property of the decedent". The proposed order (and any courtesy copy) must be single-sided. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] (The administrator was appointed with full IAEA authority on 4/19/21, but he did not claim his Letters until 5/11/21. The creditor's claims period, therefore, expired on 9/11/21, not 7/18/21.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/25/23 to avoid a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE YANG SU CHO 2015 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Beneficiary'S Petition To Remove The Trustee And Order Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code 17200
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsel (1) to update the statu of the case to the court; (2) to provide detail information regarding the LA County Court, Case No. 21STPB11229; and (3) to present proof of the written request made by Petitioner to the Trustee pursuant to Prob. Code Sec. 17200 (b)(7)(B) to show the Trustee failed to provide the information within 60 days. Said proof needs to include a copy of the written request. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KRISTINE B. MORGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the purpose of admitting the will dated 10/26/12, and to determine if the proposed self-represented executor will be capable of administering the estate without legal representation. [Probate Code Section 8225.] (Issues raised by an examiner should simply be addressed in a supplement or amendment to the petition that stands on its own, not as an answer sheet to a tentative ruling or examiner's notes.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/1/23. The Order for Probate filed on 7/25/22 set a status date of 12/12/22 for the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal, pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 8800(b). Letters have not yet been issued. If Letters are not obtained and the I&A is not filed by 2/20/23, the attorney and the personal representative must appear on 3/1/23. The Court may vacate the order appointing the administrator for failure to proceed with estate administration.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN S. JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23. No earlier availability. Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that an AMENDED petition will be heard. The amended petition, while file stamped 10/31/22, was not e-filed until 11/1/22, when it was first available for viewing. Mailing notice before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is defective. Courtesy copies of the proof of service and the proof of publication of notice of the amended petition must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 1/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

[LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proof of service has been filed. The proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 1/30/23 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
